exercises.title: Your First Output
exercises.description: Programs communicate by producing output. Use print() to display the message 'Hello, World!' exactly as shown.
exercises.starterCode: # TODO: Use print() to display: Hello, World!
hints: The print() function displays text to the screen.
Text (strings) must be wrapped in quotes: print("Hello, World!")

exercises.title: Sequential Execution
exercises.description: Python runs code line by line, top to bottom. Print three lines in order: 'Step 1', 'Step 2', 'Step 3'. Each on its own line.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print three lines in order
# Line 1: Step 1
# Line 2: Step 2
# Line 3: Step 3
hints: Python executes from top to bottom, so the order you write the print() calls determines the output order.

exercises.title: Printing Numbers and Calculations
exercises.description: print() can display numbers and math results, not just text. Print the result of 10 + 5, then the result of 3 * 7, each on its own line.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print the result of 10 + 5
# TODO: Print the result of 3 * 7
hints: You can write print(10 + 5) — no quotes needed for math.
Python evaluates the expression first, then prints the result.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: What Does a Program Do?
warmup.description: A program is a precise set of instructions. The simplest Python instruction is print(). Run this code and observe what happens.
warmup.starterCode:
# What does this program print? Run it to find out.
print("Python is")
print("really")
print("cool!")
